rulings.Case Title: EDWARD LOPEZ et al VS. AC AND S, INC. et al
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Friday, March 19, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. DEFENDANT CERTAINTEED CORPORATION's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is off calendar per dismissal filed on March 4, 20010. =(220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: JOHN AYER VS. A.W. CHESTERTON COMPANY et al
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Friday, March 19, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 2. Defendant Eaton Corporation AS SII TO CUTLER-HAMMER, INC.¿s motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant sustained its initial burden of production by presenting plaintiff¿s factually devoid discovery responses. Plaintiff¿s argument that the responses can not be used because they are unverified lacks merit. Statements contained in the special interrogatories are admissible as statements of a party or the party¿s authorized agent. =(220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: JOHN AYER VS. A.W. CHESTERTON COMPANY et al
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Friday, March 19, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 2. Defendant Buffalo Pumps, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant sustained its initial burden of production by presenting plaintiff¿s factually devoid discovery responses. Mr. Ay¿s declaration regarding asbestos content lacks foundation. =(220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: JOHN AYER VS. A.W. CHESTERTON COMPANY et al
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Friday, March 19, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 2. Defendant Sepco Corporation¿s motion for summary judgment was continuted from March 16, 2010. Defendant's motion is denied. Plaintiff raised a triable issue of exposure to an asbestos-containing product attributable to Sepco through the declaration of Mr. Throne and the prior deposition testimony of Mr. Gibson wherein he testified that 99% of all gaskets sold by Sepco Corporation contained chrysotile. Sepco¿s objections to Mr. Throne¿s declaration are overruled. =(220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: JOHN AYER VS. A.W. CHESTERTON COMPANY et al
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Friday, March 19, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 2. Defendant ITT Corporation¿s motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant shifted the burden by producing evidence of plaintiff¿s factually devoid discovery responses. While Mr. Throne testified that plaintiff was exposed to pre-existing packing on new Kennedy and Bell & Gossett valves, plaintiff¿s evidence fails to create a triable issue whether the packing contained asbestos. =(220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: JOHN AYER VS. A.W. CHESTERTON COMPANY et al
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Friday, March 19, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 2. Defendant Union Carbide Corporation¿s motion for summary adjudication is granted as to false representation (issue 1), and intentional tort (issue 2), and punitive damages (issue 3). Union Carbide shifted the burden by producing evidence of plaintiff¿s factually devoid discovery responses. Plaintiff¿s evidence fails to create a triable issue as to any of these causes of action. =(220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: JOHN AYER VS. A.W. CHESTERTON COMPANY et al
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Friday, March 19, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 2. Defendant Ingersoll-Rand Company¿s motion for summary judgment alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied. Plaintiff was not given the required 45 days notice for the motion. The continuance of the hearing date from March 9 to March 18 after plaintiff filed his objection to the motion on the grounds of insufficient notice does cure the insufficient notice. (Robinson v. Woods (2008) 168 Cal. App. 4th 1258, 1266-68). =(220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: JOHN AYER VS. A.W. CHESTERTON COMPANY et al
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Friday, March 19, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 2. Defendant Golden Gate Drywalls, Inc.¿s motion for summary adjudication is granted as to issues 1(Strict Liability) and 2 (False Representation), and denied as to issue 3 (Intentional Tort). Defendant has shown through factually devoid responses to special interrogatories that plaintiff cannot prove that defendant was a seller of asbestos-containing products. A contractor is not strictly liable for defective products it installs unless it was a seller of those products. (Monte Vista Development Corp v. Superior Court (1991) 226 Cal.App. 3d 1681) Plaintiff¿s claim for false representation under section 402B of the Restatement (Second) of Torts requires that the defendant be ¿in the business of selling chattels.¿ Defendant has not satisfied its burden of production with respect to issue 3 because a claim for fraud does not require that defendant be a seller or chattels. (Ackerman v. Schultz, (1918) 178 Cal. 190 [¿It is not essential to liability that the person charged with fraud should have received any benefit therefrom, nor where misrepresentations concern property that he should have owned it.¿]). Plaintiff ¿s request for sanctions is denied. C.R.C 2.30(c) states that ¿sanctions must not be imposed under this rule except on noticed motion by the party seeking sanctions or on the court¿s own motion after the court has provided notice and an opportunity to be heard.¿ =(220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: JOHN AYER VS. A.W. CHESTERTON COMPANY et al
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Friday, March 19, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 2. Defendant Crane Co.¿s motion for summary judgment was continued from March 16, 2010. Defendant's motion is denied. Crane Co.¿s alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to exposure (issues 1-4), false representation (issue 5), and intentional tort (issue 6), and granted as to punitive damages (issue 7). Mr. Throne¿s declaration presents a triable issue whether plaintiff was exposed to asbestos in original packing attributable to Crane Co. Crane Co.¿s request for a continuance of the hearing in order to complete Mr. Throne¿s deposition is denied. Crane Co.¿s objections to Mr. Throne¿s declaration are overruled. Crane Co. failed to shift the burden on the claims for false representation and intentional tort because it failed to propound any ¿all facts¿ interrogatories on these claims and did not ask plaintiff any questions about these claims at his deposition. Crane Co. satisfied its burden of production and plaintiff failed to create a triable issue whether Crane Co. acted with fraud, malice, or oppression by clear and convincing evidence. Because the failure of plaintiff¿s counsel to cite page and line numbers in their evidence in opposition to Crane Co.¿s motion contravenes CRC 3.1350(f) and imposes additional burdens on the court, plaintiffs¿ counsel is admonished to do so in the future. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: JOHN AYER VS. A.W. CHESTERTON COMPANY et al
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Friday, March 19, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 2. Defendant Trane US, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant shifted the burden by producing evidence of plaintiff¿s factually devoid discovery responses. While Mr. Throne testified that plaintiff was exposed to insulation on new American Standard boilers, plaintiff¿s evidence fails to create a triable issue whether the insulation contained asbestos. The declaration of Mr. Ay lacks foundation for his conclusion that the insulation on American Standard boilers more likely than not contained asbestos. =(220/HEK)
